'''Michael Darren Cassidy''' (born 3 July 1973) is the Head of youth development of the [[Wigan Warriors]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. Cassidy had a wealth of experience with the dominant [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] side of the early to mid-1990s. Cassidy is a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] representative. Cassidy's position of choice was in the {{rlp|sr}}, but he could also play as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|hk}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Cassidy has been labelled a utility player and has played wherever he has been needed.

==Background==
Cassidy was born in [[Wigan]], Lancashire, England.

==Playing career==
===Wigan===
Cassidy was born in [[Wigan]], [[Lancashire]], and started his career out at his hometown club, [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], and progressed through the academy ranks to make his senior début at the age of 18.

Cassidy was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 2–33 defeat by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1993–94 League Cup (rugby league)|1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1994, played at {{rlp|sr}} in the 40–10 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1994–95 League Cup (rugby league)|1994–95 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 28 January 1995,<ref name="28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|title=28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714151036/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|url-status=dead}}</ref> and played at {{rlp|sr}} in the 25–16 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

After the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season]] Cassidy travelled with defending champions [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to [[Brisbane]], playing from the interchange bench in their [[1994 World Club Challenge]] victory over Australian premiers, [[Brisbane Broncos]].

From the introduction of the [[Super League]] in 1996, Cassidy made 62 consecutive league appearances for Wigan until he received a suspension in August 1998.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Six-match suspension for 'vicious' Cassidy |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-sixmatch-suspension-for-vicious-cassidy-1174203.html |website=The Independent |access-date=22 January 2024 |date=25 August 1998}}</ref> He returned from suspension to play for Wigan as a substitute in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sport: Rugby League  Robinson's run helps Wigan to glory |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_league/200798.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=22 January 2024 |date=24 October 1998}}</ref>

Cassidy played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].

Cassidy served a total of 14 years at the Wigan club, enjoying a testimonial in 2000. Cassidy played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] loss to the [[Bradford Bulls]].

Cassidy played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to the [[Bradford Bulls]].
Cassidy was named in the Wigan team of the decade and was acknowledged as one of the game's best utility players.<ref name="Team of the Decade">{{cite web
 |url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/MidContent.asp?cid=165 
 |title=Team of the Decade 
 |publisher=BBC 
 |date=20 July 2007 
 |access-date=20 July 2007 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070808183623/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/MidContent.asp?cid=165 
 |archive-date=8 August 2007 
 |url-status=dead 
}}</ref>

===Widnes===
Cassidy joined [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in 2005, earning the 'Player of the Year' award in his first season at the [[Halton Stadium]].<ref name="Mick Cassidy">{{cite web
 |url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=5 
 |title=Mick Cassidy 
 |publisher=Widnes 
 |date=20 July 2007 
 |access-date=20 July 2007 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201240/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=5 
 |archive-date=27 September 2007 
 |url-status=dead 
}}</ref> He could not, however save them from relegation from [[Super League]] in 2005's [[Super League X]]. Cassidy represented the Irish side on numerous occasions over several years. Cassidy finished his playing career with Cumbrian side [[Barrow Raiders]], helping the club win promotion from [[RFL League 1|National League Two]] in 2008.

===International career===
Cassidy was part of the [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="World Cup 1995 Details">{{cite web|url=http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm |title=World Cup 1995 Details |publisher=Hunter |date=20 July 2007 |access-date=20 July 2007 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070929104732/http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm |archive-date=29 September 2007 }}</ref> He was selected to play for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] Final on the reserve bench but [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] won the match and retained the Cup.

In the 1997 post season, Cassidy was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in the first game of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].

Cassidy later switched allegiance through ancestry to play for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]]. He was ruled out of the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] through injury.<ref name="Irish World Cup setback">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/923000.stm|title=Irish World Cup setback|publisher=BBC|date=13 September 2000|access-date=20 July 2007}}</ref> He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref>
